claims.claim: Under uniform traffic, HRW reduces the max-to-mean load ratio from 64.0 (no breaker) to 1.035, comparable to random routing (1.031), while preserving deterministic client-side agreement and incurring only ~1.5% key remapping on membership changes versus ~98.5% for modulo hashing.

claims.claim: However, under a single dominant hot key absorbing 90% of traffic, HRW's deterministic affinity maps that key to one backend, yielding max/mean ≈ 57.8—no meaningful improvement over the no-breaker baseline.

evidence_refs.quote: We do not assume that HRW solves all load-balancing problems. In particular, deterministic key-to-backend affinity implies that a single dominant key cannot be split across backends without changing the assignment unit. We evaluate this trade-off explicitly.
